title: National Identity Card — New Application
description: Obtain your first Smart NIC. All Sri Lankan citizens aged 16 and above are eligible.

offices.name: DRP Head Office
offices.address: 10th Floor, Suhurupaya, Sri Subhuthipura Road, Battaramulla

offices.name: Divisional Secretariat Offices
offices.address: Island-wide — primary service points for NIC applications

documents_required: Original Birth Certificate
ICAO-standard photograph obtained within the last 6 months (from authorized provider)
Completed Form D.R.P/1 — download from drp.gov.lk or collect at Divisional Secretariat
Parent/Guardian's NIC (if applicant is under 18)
School certificate or principal's letter (if aged 16–17)
Receipt of application fee payment
steps: Download Form D.R.P/1 from drp.gov.lk or collect it at your nearest Divisional Secretariat
Gather all required documents (originals + photocopies as requested)
Visit your nearest Divisional Secretariat or DRP Head Office (Battaramulla) and submit the application
Photograph and fingerprints are captured on-site
Normal service: NIC is mailed to your address within approximately 3 months
One Day Service: NIC can be collected within about 3 hours (additional fee applies)

fees.late_application_penalty: LKR 2,500 (if not applied within prescribed time after age 15)

processing_time: Normal: ~3 months (mailed). One Day Service: ~3 hours
tips: DRP Head Office is at Suhurupaya, Battaramulla — not the old Maligawatte address
First-time NIC fee is LKR 200 — not free
Divisional Secretariats handle most applications island-wide
One Day Service costs LKR 2,000 extra and is processed in about 3 hours
common_mistakes: Going to the outdated Maligawatte Colombo 10 address
Bringing a photocopy of the birth certificate instead of the original
Using Form names incorrectly — first application uses Form D.R.P/1
Expecting free issuance — government fee is LKR 200 for first issue

title: NIC Replacement (Lost, Damaged, or Change of Details)
description: Replace a lost, damaged, or stolen NIC, or update your name, address, or other personal details.

offices.name: DRP Head Office
offices.address: 10th Floor, Suhurupaya, Sri Subhuthipura Road, Battaramulla

documents_required: Completed application for replacement (lost/damaged) or amendment (change of particulars) — from drp.gov.lk or DS office
Police report (mandatory for lost NIC — file at nearest police station immediately)
Original damaged NIC (if damaged, not lost)
Original Birth Certificate
ICAO-standard photograph within 6 months
Affidavit or supporting documents as required for loss reports
steps: If lost: file a police report at your nearest police station without delay
Download or collect the correct replacement/amendment form from drp.gov.lk or Divisional Secretariat
Gather documents including police report (for loss) and birth certificate
Visit Divisional Secretariat or DRP Head Office (Battaramulla) and submit application with fee
Normal service: receive NIC by mail in ~3 months; One Day Service: ~3 hours
processing_time: Normal: ~3 months (mailed). One Day Service: ~3 hours
tips: File the police report on the same day you discover the NIC is lost
Keep a photocopy of your NIC at home — it makes the replacement process much faster
Use Form NIC 2 for lost/damaged, and NIC 3 only for changing details on an existing NIC
common_mistakes: Applying for replacement without first filing a police report (for lost NICs)
Using Form NIC 1 (new application) instead of Form NIC 2 (replacement)

title: Passport — New Application
description: Apply for your first Sri Lankan passport. Valid 10 years for adults; children under 16 may receive 3-year or 10-year validity.
documents_required: National Identity Card (NIC) — original (must attend in person)
Birth Certificate — original and photocopy
Digital photograph from an authorized photo studio (printed photos are NOT accepted since August 2015)
Completed passport application form (K35A) — from immigration.gov.lk, Department, Regional Offices, or Divisional Secretariat
Government fee payment receipt from the Department cashier
Marriage Certificate (if applying in married name)
Parent's NIC + child's Birth Certificate (for applicants under 16)
steps: Obtain your digital photograph from an authorized photo studio listed on immigration.gov.lk
Download Form K35A and complete it — available at immigration.gov.lk, Head Office, Regional Offices, or Divisional Secretariats
Pay the applicable government fee at the Department of Immigration and Emigration cashier (Normal or One-Day service)
Attend Head Office (Battaramulla), a Regional Office, or eligible Divisional Secretariat in person — fingerprints are captured on-site
For Normal service: collect passport after processing (approximately 30 working days for adults)
For One-Day urgent service: only available at Head Office Battaramulla and Jaffna Regional Office — submit before cutoff time on weekdays

fees.child_under_16_normal: LKR 3,000 (3-year or 10-year validity per parents' request)

processing_time: Normal: ~30 working days (adults). One-Day urgent: same day (Head Office Battaramulla or Jaffna only, weekday cutoff applies)
tips: Head Office moved from Colombo 10 to Suhurupaya, Battaramulla — do NOT go to the old Ananda Rajakaruna Mawatha address
One-Day (urgent) service is ONLY at Battaramulla Head Office and Jaffna Regional Office — other regional offices are Normal service only
You must appear in person for fingerprint capture — no walk-in without completing the application process
Call 1962 or check immigration.gov.lk before visiting — fees and cutoff times can change by gazette
common_mistakes: Going to the old Colombo 10 office address — the Head Office is now in Battaramulla
Bringing printed photographs instead of using an authorized digital photo studio
Expecting one-day service at Kandy/Matara/Kurunegala — urgent service is Battaramulla and Jaffna only
Using outdated fee amounts (current adult fees are LKR 10,000 normal / LKR 20,000 one-day, not LKR 3,500)

title: Passport — Renewal
description: Renew an expiring or expired Sri Lankan passport. Same fees as a new application. Adults receive 10-year validity; children under 16 may receive 3-year or 10-year validity.

offices.name: Regional Offices
offices.address: Kandy, Matara, Kurunegala, Vavuniya, Jaffna — verify at immigration.gov.lk

documents_required: Current or expired passport — original (returned cancelled after renewal)
National Identity Card (NIC) — original (must attend in person for fingerprints)
Digital photograph from an authorized photo studio (printed photos NOT accepted)
Completed passport application form (K35A) for renewal
Government fee payment receipt from the Department cashier
steps: Obtain digital photograph from an authorized photo studio on immigration.gov.lk
Complete Form K35A — same form used for new applications and renewals
Pay renewal fee at the Department cashier: LKR 10,000 (Normal) or LKR 20,000 (One-Day urgent)
Attend Head Office Battaramulla, a Regional Office, or eligible Divisional Secretariat in person with old passport and NIC
Biometrics (fingerprints) captured on-site — applicant must be present
Collect new passport on the issued date; old passport returned cancelled
processing_time: Normal: ~30 working days. One-Day urgent: same day at Battaramulla Head Office or Jaffna only
tips: Renewal fees are the same as new passport fees — LKR 10,000 normal, LKR 20,000 one-day for adults
Expired passports can still be renewed — there is no time limit on renewal eligibility
One-day service only at Battaramulla and Jaffna — plan ahead if travelling soon
Valid visas in the old passport remain valid after renewal — they are noted on the new passport
common_mistakes: Using outdated Colombo 10 address — Head Office is at Suhurupaya, Battaramulla
Quoting old fees (LKR 3,500/7,000) — current adult fees are LKR 10,000 and LKR 20,000
Bringing printed photos — only authorized digital studio photos are accepted
Expecting urgent same-day service outside Battaramulla or Jaffna

title: Driving License — Learner's (Provisional) License
description: First step to a full driving licence. Register for the written test from age 17; learner permit valid up to 18 months. Minimum 3 months on learner permit before practical test for light vehicles (age 18+).

offices.name: Head Office
offices.address: 341, Dr. Danister De Silva Mawatha (Alvitigala Mawatha), Narahenpita, Colombo 05

documents_required: National Identity Card (NIC) — original (must be 17+ to sit written test; 18+ for practical)
Medical fitness certificate from National Transport Medical Institute (NTMI) — valid 6 months
Completed DMT application forms
Payment receipt for learner permit / new licence levy
steps: Book appointment at dmtappointments.dmt.gov.lk — select new driving licence
Obtain medical certificate from NTMI (list on dmt.gov.lk) — valid 6 months
Attend appointment and pass the computer-based written theory test (Highway Code)
Pay levy: LKR 2,500 (1 class), LKR 3,000 (2 classes), or LKR 3,500 (3+ classes) — normal service
Receive learner's permit — display 'L' plates when practising
Hold learner permit minimum 3 months before practical test (light vehicles); then book practical test for full licence

fees.same_day_service_surcharge: Add LKR 1,000 per category (e.g. 1 class same-day LKR 3,500)

processing_time: Same day upon passing written test
tips: Written test available from age 17; practical test for light vehicles from age 18 after 3 months on learner permit
Medical certificate must be from NTMI — not any private doctor
Learner permit valid up to 18 months
Heavy vehicle classes have higher age minimums (20–21) — see dmt.gov.lk
common_mistakes: Thinking you need 6 months on learner permit — minimum is 3 months for light vehicles
Medical certificate from non-NTMI doctor — will be rejected
Underestimating theory test — study the Highway Code thoroughly

title: Driving License — Full License
description: Obtain a full driving licence after holding a valid learner's permit for at least 3 months (light vehicles) and passing the practical driving test.

offices.name: Head Office
offices.address: 341, Dr. Danister De Silva Mawatha, Narahenpita, Colombo 05

documents_required: Valid learner's permit (minimum 3 months old for light vehicle classes)
National Identity Card (NIC) — original
Medical fitness certificate from NTMI — issued within last 6 months
Roadworthy vehicle for the practical test class
Payment receipt for applicable DMT levies
steps: Confirm learner's permit is at least 3 months old (light vehicles) and you meet minimum age (18 for light vehicles)
Book appointment at dmtappointments.dmt.gov.lk
Obtain fresh NTMI medical certificate if expired
Attend with your vehicle and pass the practical road test with DMT examiner
Pay fees and receive smart-card driving licence same day if passed

fees.note: Full licence issue levies follow same schedule as new licence application — see dmt.gov.lk Schedule I

processing_time: Same day if practical test passed
tips: Minimum learner period is 3 months for light vehicles — not 6 months
Vehicle must be roadworthy — lights, horn, tyres checked before test
Enrol at a registered driving school to learn examiner expectations
common_mistakes: Appearing before 3-month learner period ends
Vehicle fails roadworthiness check on test day
Using expired NTMI medical certificate

title: Driving License — Renewal
description: Renew or extend the validity period of your driving licence.

offices.name: Head Office
offices.address: 341, Dr. Danister De Silva Mawatha, Narahenpita, Colombo 05

documents_required: Current or expired driving licence — original
National Identity Card (NIC) or valid passport with NIC number
Medical fitness certificate from NTMI — within last 6 months (required for renewal/extension)
Payment receipt for renewal levy
steps: Book appointment at dmtappointments.dmt.gov.lk — Driving Licence Renewal
Obtain NTMI medical certificate if required
Attend DMT on appointment date with licence and NIC
Pay renewal levy and collect renewed smart-card licence — usually same day
tips: Renew before expiry to avoid LKR 500 lapsed-period penalty (if lapsed ≤1 year)
NTMI medical certificate required — valid 6 months
Driving with expired licence is an offence
common_mistakes: Not bringing NTMI medical certificate
Ignoring lapsed-period penalty when licence expired
Medical certificate from non-NTMI provider

title: Birth Certificate — Certified Copy
description: Obtain a certified copy of your birth certificate. This is one of the most fundamental documents — required for NIC, passport, marriage, and more.

offices.name: Divisional Secretariat Registrar Division
offices.address: Registrar division at DS where birth was registered — or any DS if record is in eBMD

documents_required: National Identity Card (NIC) of the applicant
Birth certificate number and date of registration (if known — reduces fee)
Completed application form (at DS or online eBMD)
steps: Check if your record is in eBMD (1960–2020) at nearest Divisional Secretariat
Option A — Online: apply at https://online.ebmd.rgd.gov.lk, pay via link sent within 2 days, receive by post or collect at DS (~5 working days)
Option B — In person: visit DS Registrar Division with NIC and pay fee at counter (or BOC Battaramulla acct 7039827 with receipt)
Collect certified copy — same day at DS if record is in database

fees.certified_copy_search_required: LKR 250 (first copy if number/date unknown; additional copies LKR 120)

processing_time: Same day at DS (in database); ~5 working days online
tips: RGD Head Office moved to Battaramulla — not Deans Road Colombo 10
Official fee is LKR 120 per copy when number and date are known
eBMD online portal: online.ebmd.rgd.gov.lk
Records from 1960–2020 may be available at any DS via eBMD
common_mistakes: Quoting outdated LKR 200–300 fees — official fee is LKR 120
Going to old RGD Colombo 10 address
Not knowing certificate number — triggers LKR 250 search fee

title: Marriage Registration & Certificate
description: Register a civil marriage and obtain a marriage certificate through the Registrar General's Department.
documents_required: NICs of both parties — originals
Birth Certificates of both parties — originals
Two witnesses aged 18+ with their NICs
Divorce Certificate or Death Certificate of deceased spouse (if previously married)
Notice of Marriage in duplicate — certified
steps: Submit written Notice of Marriage to the Marriage Registrar at your Divisional Secretariat
Pay notice entry fee (LKR 120) — 14 days must elapse before registration
Both parties and witnesses appear on registration date (within 3 months of notice)
Pay solemnization fee (LKR 900) and receive Marriage Certificate
Register marriage at DS Registrar office — certificate issued same day
processing_time: Certificate on registration day (after 14-day notice period)
tips: 14-day notice is legally required — special license (LKR 120) needed to marry sooner
Registration must occur within 3 months of notice or a new notice is required
Certified copies of marriage certificate available via eBMD (LKR 120 if details known)
common_mistakes: Not allowing 14 days after notice
Missing the 3-month registration window after notice
Witnesses without NICs on the day

title: Police Clearance Certificate
description: Official certificate confirming you have no criminal record in Sri Lanka. Required for overseas employment, visa applications, migration, and certain government and private jobs.
documents_required: Clear scan of National Identity Card (both sides)
Valid passport copy (especially for overseas purposes)
Correct email address for status updates
Debit/credit card for online payment
steps: Visit www.police.lk → E-Services → Clearance Certificates → Apply Online
Complete the online application with accurate NIC and passport details
Pay fee online by card — applications with invalid NIC/passport scans are rejected without refund
Background check conducted — certificate issued to Sri Lankan address by registered post
Overseas residents: fee LKR 5,100; may apply via embassy or online per police.lk instructions

fees.online_application: As per police.lk payment gateway (overseas applicants: LKR 5,100 cited on police.lk)
fees.local_employment_certificate_at_station: LKR 25 (separate local employment certificate — different service)

processing_time: Approximately 14 days (overseas); varies for local online applications
tips: Clearance applications are online-only via police.lk — walk-in at HQ is not the primary process
Use a valid Sri Lankan postal address for delivery by registered post
WhatsApp support: 071 8592990 | clearance@police.gov.lk
Some countries require apostille after clearance — Ministry of Foreign Affairs
common_mistakes: Blurry NIC/passport scans — application rejected without refund
Wrong email address — miss rejection notices
Confusing local LKR 25 employment certificate with formal Police Clearance Certificate

title: Vehicle First Registration
description: Register a brand new or imported vehicle in Sri Lanka for the first time to receive a registration number and the vehicle registration book (log book).

offices.name: Head Office
offices.address: 341, Dr. Danister De Silva Mawatha, Narahenpita, Colombo 05

documents_required: Invoice from the dealer or supplier
Customs Entry (CE) form — for imported vehicles only
National Identity Card (NIC) of the owner
Valid Insurance Certificate (at minimum third-party from an approved insurer)
Completed vehicle registration application form
Emission Test (VET) Certificate if applicable
steps: Book an appointment at dmtappointments.dmt.gov.lk — select 'Vehicle First Registration'
Ensure insurance is purchased before your visit — DMT will not register without it
For imported vehicles: complete customs clearance first and have the CE form ready
Bring the vehicle to the DMT office on your appointment date for inspection
The DMT assigns a registration number and issues number plates and the log book

fees.varies: Depends on vehicle type, engine capacity, and age — check dmt.gov.lk for the current fee schedule

tips: TIN (Tax Identification Number) may be required for vehicle registration transfers — check dmt.gov.lk
Reputable dealers often handle first registration for new vehicles
Insurance from approved Sri Lankan insurer is mandatory before registration
common_mistakes: Arriving without insurance — registration will not proceed
Not completing customs clearance before the DMT appointment for imports

title: Vehicle Revenue License (Annual Road Tax)
description: Annual revenue licence (road tax disc) required for all motor vehicles on public roads. Renew annually — peak demand December–January.
department: Department of Motor Traffic (for online) / Local Authority (Municipal Council, UC, Pradeshiya Sabha)

offices.name: Local Municipal Council or Pradeshiya Sabha

documents_required: Previous year's Revenue License (OR) or the Vehicle Registration Certificate (log book)
Valid Emission Test (VET) certificate — from an approved testing centre
Valid Insurance Certificate (minimum third-party)
Payment for the annual revenue license fee
steps: Get the Emission Test (VET) done first at a certified VET station — not all garages are approved
Renew your vehicle insurance (must be current)
Visit your local Municipal Council, Pradeshiya Sabha, or Divisional Secretariat with all documents
Pay the revenue license fee at the counter
Receive the revenue license disc — display it on your windscreen immediately

fees.varies: LKR 2,000–15,000+ depending on vehicle type and engine capacity

tips: Online renewal available at revenuelicence.dmt.gov.lk in many areas
Get VET emission test and insurance renewed before applying
Renew early in December to avoid January queues and penalties
common_mistakes: Getting the emission test at an uncertified garage — it will not be accepted
Waiting until after January 31 — fines for overdue revenue licenses are significant
Forgetting to renew insurance before going for the revenue license

title: Grama Niladhari (GN) Certificates
description: Various certificates issued by your local Grama Niladhari (GN) officer. These are frequently required as supporting documents for NIC, passport, job applications, and bank accounts.

offices.name: Your local Grama Niladhari office
offices.address: Ask at your nearest Divisional Secretariat to find your GN division

documents_required: National Identity Card (NIC) — original
Written request letter stating the certificate type and purpose
Any supporting documents relevant to the specific certificate (e.g., birth certificate for residency)
available_certificates: Residency / Domicile Certificate (proof you live in that GN division)
Moral Character / Behaviour Certificate (confirms good conduct)
Land Ownership Certificate (for property within the GN area)
steps: Find out your GN officer's visiting days and hours — usually 2–3 days per week at the GN office
Visit the GN office with your NIC and a written request
State the type of certificate and the purpose clearly
The GN officer may visit your home to verify (for poverty or residency certificates)
Collect the signed certificate — usually within 1–7 days
tips: Find your GN officer's schedule first — they visit the office on specific days, not every day
The Residency Certificate is one of the most commonly requested — keep a few copies handy
GN certificates often have a validity period (e.g., 3 months) — get them closer to when you need them
common_mistakes: Going to the Divisional Secretariat office instead of the GN office — different levels of government
Not knowing which GN division you belong to — ask the DS office to look it up by your NIC

title: Voter Registration
description: Register as an elector or update your registration when you move. Annual revision cycle with enumeration each year.
documents_required: National Identity Card (NIC)
Enumeration form (BC form) — completed during house-to-house enumeration
Claim forms A/B if correcting the electoral register during exhibition period
steps: During annual revision, enumerator visits and you complete BC form with household details
Must be 18 by February 1 of the revision year and normally resident at the address
Provisional register exhibited in September for 28 days — check elections.gov.lk
Submit claim/objection forms (A, B, C, D) during exhibition if details are wrong
Supplementary registration available for those turning 18 mid-year (Feb–Jan windows)

fees.electoral_register_extract: Prescribed fee at District Elections Office

processing_time: Annual cycle — enumeration mid-year, register certified before elections
tips: Must be 18 by February 1 and normally resident at the address on that date
Check your registration online at elections.gov.lk during register exhibition
Update registration after moving — otherwise you vote at your old polling station
common_mistakes: Not completing BC form during enumeration visit
Missing the September exhibition period to file corrections
Assuming NIC alone registers you — enumeration/claim process is required
